light_pressure_read.read: Light and pressure do not strongly push the outlook either way.

stocking_read.read: Recent stocking supports eggs, worms, and attractor nymphs.

angler_analysis.expected_trout_behavior: Expect trout to favor the most comfortable feeding lanes while staying close to cover when light is high.
angler_analysis.presentation_style: Prioritize clean drifts, smaller natural patterns, and stocked-trout attractors where appropriate.

fly_reasoning.egg_pattern: Recent stocking makes eggs a logical confidence fly.
fly_reasoning.squirmy_worm: A simple stocked-trout attractor and good dirty-water option.
fly_reasoning.duracell: A strong attractor nymph when fish will move a little but are not fully aggressive.

generated_text.quick_read: Trout fishing on the Toccoa River is fair to good with a quality score of 6.0. Recent stocking enhances opportunities using eggs, worms, and attractor nymphs.
generated_text.best_window: Day 2 morning offers the best fishing due to moderate sky cover, high humidity, low rain probability, and cool air temperatures, aligning with trout comfort and feeding activity.
generated_text.current_conditions: Current flow is 230 cfs with a gage height of 1.21 ft. Water temperature and turbidity data are unavailable at this time.
generated_text.weather_setup: Current weather shows partly cloudy skies with 29.6% average cover, stable pressure at 30.06 inHg, west winds averaging 3.5 mph with gusts near 13.8 mph, and a 63% chance of light rain totaling 0.1 inches expected.
generated_text.what_this_means: Trout are likely positioned in runs, seams, and around structure, favoring comfortable feeding lanes and cover. Use a balanced nymph approach with prioritized clean drifts and smaller natural patterns using Egg Pattern, Squirmy Worm, or Duracell flies to match the recent stocking. Caution with changing light and weather conditions as trout may shift positions.
